12 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Pentagon Says Cost Of Iran War About $29 Billion RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- The Pentagon says that the United States' war with Iran has cost about $29 billion, up $4 billion from an estimate made two weeks earlier.
  • Pentagon lowballs Iran war cost at $29 billion as real toll could hit $1 trillion Press TV 12 May 2026 -- A senior Pentagon official has provided an updated estimate regarding the financial implications of the war on Iran, claiming that the war has already cost the United States approximately $29 billion since its onset on February 28, while estimates point to higher figures.
  • Trump admits US sent weapons to fuel riots, terrorism inside Iran Press TV 12 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has again admitted to Washington's support for armed riots and terrorist activities inside Iran amid continued aggressive American measures targeting the nation.

  • US envoy admits Israel secretly sent Iron Dome to UAE during war on Iran Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Israel secretly deployed Iron Dome missile-defense batteries and troops to the United Arab Emirates during the war against Iran, says the US ambassador to Israel, revealing deepening military cooperation between the occupying regime and the monarchy.

  • Second Qatari LNG Tanker Crosses Strait of Hormuz RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- A second Qatari liquefied natural gas (LNG) tanker has successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz under a special arrangement involving Iran and Pakistan, highlighting efforts to maintain energy shipments despite regional conflict risks.
  • May 12: Iran's strategic edge widens as top officials warn US against new military adventurism Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Iran's strategic upper hand is becoming increasingly visible, as the United States finds itself in a ring of fire with no viable options or leverage to advance its agenda.
  • Strait of Hormuz now a 500-kilometer operational 'crescent': IRGC Navy Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has announced a major strategic expansion of Iran's defensive perimeter in the Strait of Hormuz, warning that it will not allow any violation of the country's territorial waters or national interests.
  • May 11: Iran reaffirms military readiness, vows 'decisive, crushing' response to any new aggression Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Iranian leadership on Monday reaffirmed the country's military preparedness and political cohesion, warning that any further aggression by the United States or its allies will be met with a "decisive and crushing" response.

  • UK to contribute drones, jets and warship to Multinational Mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz UK MOD 12 May 2026 -- Defence Secretary announced UK contribution to Strait of Hormuz mission at virtual summit of Defence Ministers today.
  • US, UK, Australia Tighten Sanctions On Iran Over Oil Trade, Security Threats RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- The US Treasury Department has sanctioned 12 individuals and entities accused of helping the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) facilitate the sale and shipment of Iranian oil to China, as Washington intensifies its "maximum pressure" campaign against Tehran.

  • Trump: '100 Percent' Confident Iran Will Stop Uranium Enrichment RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has said that he is "100 percent" confident that Iran would stop enriching uranium and hand over its stockpile to the United States.
  • Trump dismissed Iran's response since it wasn't a 'letter of surrender': Official Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The United States rejected Iran's latest peace proposal solely because it was not a "letter of surrender," a senior Iranian diplomat has said, noting that Washington seeks to impose its will through intimidation and pressure rather than building genuine peace.
  • US intelligence says Iran retains significant missile capabilities: Report Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Classified US intelligence assessments indicate that Iran continues to maintain substantial missile capabilities, despite repeated claims by Donald Trump's administration that the Islamic Republic's military has been "shattered," a report says.
  • Trump Says No Need to Rush When It Comes to Iran Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that Washington does not have to rush while trying to settle the conflict with Iran.

  • Lawmaker Warns Iran Could Enrich Uranium To Weapons Grade If Attacked RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- An Iranian lawmaker says that Tehran could enrich uranium to 90 percent purity -- a level associated with nuclear weapons capability -- if the country comes under attack again.
  • Iran FM: US's approach main obstacle to ending war Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi identifies the US's maximalist approach as well as its other instances of counterproductive attitude as the main obstacles to ending the situation that has arisen out of unprovoked aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran could enrich uranium to weapons grade if attacked, lawmaker warns Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 May 2026 -- Iranian parliamentary spokesman Ebrahim Rezaei said on Tuesday that the country could enrich uranium up to 90% ‌purity, a level considered weapons-grade, if Iran is attacked again, according to Reuters.
  • Accept Iran's rights or face repeated military defeats: Defense Ministry Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Iran's Defense Ministry says the American-Zionist enemy must accept the legitimate and definitive rights of the Iranian nation, or expect to suffer repeated defeats both on the diplomatic front and on the battlefield.
  • Iran demands end to war before nuclear talks as FM Araghchi set to attend India BRICS meeting Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei has highlighted the Islamic Republic's firm stance on nuclear negotiations with the United States, saying ending the war and the removal of the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz are prerequisites to any discussions.
  • IRGC's Tehran command holds major drill to prepare for any US-Israeli aggression Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The IRGC's Greater Tehran Command has conducted a large-scale military exercise focusing on readiness to confront enemy forces in any terrain.
  • Iran's deputy foreign minister blasts US-proposed UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Iran has sharply condemned Washington's latest attempt to push a draft resolution through the UN Security Council on the Strait of Hormuz, describing it as a blatant effort to distort facts and shield the real aggressors.

  • China Prioritizes Regime Survival In Iran, Analyst Moriyasu Says Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- The war with Tehran is casting a shadow over US President Donald Trump's high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing later this week, where Iran is expected to emerge as one of the most sensitive issues on the agenda.
  • China rejects groundless and unsubstantiated accusations, FM on Netanyahu's claims that China provides support to Iran on missile manufacturing components Global Times 12 May 2026 -- When asked to comment on claims by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in an interview that China had provided certain amount of support to Iran regarding particular components of missile manufacturing, adding that he didn't like it, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday that China has made clear its position on multiple occasions.
  • China rejects Israel's 'groundless' allegation of missile support for Iran Press TV 12 May 2026 -- China has rejected Israel's claims that Beijing provided support to Iran in manufacturing missiles.

  • Official Response to CBS Report on Iranian Aircraft in Pakistan Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 May 2026 -- Pakistan categorically rejects the CBS News report regarding the presence of Iranian aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase as misleading and sensationalized. Such speculative narratives appear aimed at undermining ongoing efforts for regional stability and peace.
  • Mediator Or Strategic Opportunist? Pakistan Faces Scrutiny Over Iran Role RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- Pakistan's role as a mediator between Iran and the United States has come under renewed scrutiny after reports that Iranian aircraft were parked at a military base just outside Rawalpindi, raising questions about Islamabad's balancing act in the unfolding conflict.

  • UAE carried out 'covert' attacks against Iran during US-Israeli aggression: Report Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates conducted a series of "covert" attacks against Iran during the latest bout of US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic, according to a report.

  • Saudi Arabia carried out 'covert' strikes on Iran amid US-Israeli aggression in March: Report Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Saudi Arabia launched a series of "covert" airstrikes against Iran in late March before "threatening" the Islamic Republic with more aggression, a report says.

  • S. Korea to participate in defense ministers' meeting on Hormuz after ship blast Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 May 2026 -- South Korea will participate in a multinational defense ministerial meeting on the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, Yonhap News Agency reported citing local officials, as Seoul weighs its response following the recent attack on a Korean-operated cargo vessel in the strategic waterway.
  • Nat'l security chief blasts attack on vessel Namu, pledges action KOREA.net 12 May 2026 -- National Security Office Director Wi Sung-lac on May 11 blasted the recent attack on the domestic civilian vessel HMM Namu in the Strait of Hormuz, saying, "The Korean government strongly condemns this, maintaining the position that attacks on civilian vessels like the Namu cannot be justified or tolerated."
  • Autonomous sanctions targeting Iran's campaign of oppression and destabilisation Australia MFA 12 May 2026 -- The Australian Government is today imposing further targeted financial sanctions and travel bans on Iranian individuals and entities in response to the regime's ongoing brutal oppression of its people and destabilisation of the region.

  • As Iran Talks Stall, Markets Bet on Diplomacy, Experts Say RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- The latest exchange between Washington and Tehran over a proposed path toward de-escalation has raised new questions about what comes next in the growing crisis around the Strait of Hormuz.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War Department Multiyear Purchases Spur Industrial Base Investments DoD News 12 May 2026 -- War Department investments in the fiscal year 2026 budget and promised in the FY27 budget have heartened companies within the defense industrial base to invest in their own capabilities and to grow their capacity to produce.
  • Joint Interagency Task Force Enables Standardized Counter-UAS Assessment DoD News 12 May 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 recently supported the Army's assessment of emerging counter-unmanned aerial system capabilities during Project Flytrap 5.0, a training exercise currently underway in Lithuania.
  • Pentagon Chief Says He Approved Sending More Personnel to Ukraine to Study UAV Use Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday that he had approved sending additional personnel to Ukraine to study the use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in both offensive and defensive operations.
  • Air Force, Boeing accelerate KC-46 upgrades to target readiness US Air Force 12 May 2026 -- The U.S. Air Force and the Boeing Company have come to terms for a plan to improve the readiness of the KC-46 Pegasus tanker and accelerate capability delivery to the fleet.
  • AFOSI Center stands up new sUAS cell amid drone surge Air Force Office of Special Investigations 12 May 2026 -- The Air Force Office of Special Investigations Center recently stood up its new small unmanned aircraft systems (sUAS) integration cell to bolster support for detachments responding to drone-related incidents.
  • Navy's Directed Energy Systems Integration Lab to Train Sailors on Laser Weapons US Navy 12 May 2026 -- Sailors can now get certified in operating and maintaining the fleet's most prevalent directed energy system through a new training program at Naval Surface Warfare Center, Port Hueneme Division (NSWC PHD)'s Directed Energy Systems Integration Laboratory (DESIL), with the first course completed in late March.
  • 2026 NAVSUP Commander's Conference Reinforces Strategy and Unity US Navy 12 May 2026 -- Leaders from across the Naval Supply Systems Command (NAVSUP) enterprise gathered onboard Naval Base Point Loma in San Diego from May 5-7 for the 2026 NAVSUP Commander's Conference to align strategic goals with the Chief of Naval Operations' fighting instructions.
  • NAVSCIATTS Redesignated as Special Boat Training Command - Marking a New Era in Maritime Security Cooperation US Navy 12 May 2026 -- The Naval Small Craft Instruction and Technical Training School (NAVSCIATTS) was officially redesignated as the Special Boat Training Command (SBTC) March 23, 2026, marking a pivotal evolution for one of the U.S. Department of the Navy's most enduring security cooperation assets.
  • Northern Europe defense conference builds on longstanding partnerships for security and modern warfighting EUCOM 12 May 2026 -- U.S. Air Force General Alexus Grynkewich, commander of U.S. European Command and Supreme Allied Commander Europe, co-chaired the 2026 Northern Europe Chiefs of Defense Conference here alongside General Raimundas Vaikšnoras, Chief of Defence of Lithuania on May 11 and 12. The event brought together senior military leaders from across the region and Allied nations to strengthen combined military operations and modern warfighting, enabling Allies to enhance their contributions to Europe's conventional defense.

  • Combined Space Operations (CSpO) Initiative Principals' Board Meets to Advance Combined Operations Efforts Government of Norway 12 May 2026 -- The Combined Space Operations (CSpO) Initiative Principals' Board convened on April 17, 2026, in Colorado Springs, Colorado.

  • French Rafales supporting NATO Baltic Air Policing mission in Lithuania NATO Allied Air Command 12 May 2026 -- Around 100 French Air and Space Force aviators have been deployed to Lithuania since April 1 for NATO's Baltic Air Policing 71 (BAP-71) mission, flying four Rafales from Šiauliai Air Base.
  • NATO Secretary General visits Montenegro and praises its contributions to NATO and stability in the Western Balkans NATO 12 May 2026 -- On Tuesday (12 May 2026), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte visited Montenegro and met with Prime Minister Milojko Spajić and President Jakov Milatović. They discussed transatlantic security, stability in the Western Balkans, and preparations for the upcoming NATO Summit in Ankara.
  • NATO-Serbia Exercise 2026 Commences NATO Allied Joint Force Command (JFC) Naples 12 May 2026 -- Today (12 May 2026) featured the opening ceremony for the first NATO-Serbia military exercise, at Military Base South and Borovac Training Area, in Serbia. The 2026 NATO-Serbia exercise (NSE26) will run until 23 May 2026.

  • Statement by the President on the successful test launch of Sarmat missile President of Russia 12 May 2026 -- Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Russian Federation Armed Forces Vladimir Putin received a report via videoconference from Commander of the Strategic Missile Forces Sergei Karakayev on the successful test of the Sarmat missile.
  • Strategic Missile Forces successfully launch Sarmat heavy liquid-fuelled intercontinental ballistic missile Russia MoD 12 May 2026 -- The Strategic Missile Forces successfully launched the Sarmat heavy liquid-fuelled intercontinental ballistic missile. Sergei Karakayev, the Commander of the Strategic Missile Forces, reported to Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Russian Armed Forces Vladimir Putin on the event.
  • Putin confirms Sarmat ICBM test success, eyes 2026 combat deployment Press TV 12 May 2026 -- President Vladimir Putin announced that Russia has successfully tested a new nuclear-capable intercontinental ballistic missile, which he described as the most powerful missile system in the world.
  • Putin Calls Russia's Latest Sarmat Missile Most Powerful System in World Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- Russia's latest Sarmat missile is the most powerful missile system in the world, with a total capacity of more than four times that of any Western equivalent, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Tuesday.
  • Straight Out of the Silo: Russia's Super-Weapon Sarmat Aces Test Launch Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- The Russian strategic missile forces conducted a successful test launch of the latest Sarmat missile, commander of the strategic missile forces, Colonel General Sergei Karakaev said on Tuesday.

  • The Ukrainian Delegation Discussed Military and Humanitarian Support for Ukraine during Meetings with the President and Government Officials of Lithuania President of Ukraine 12 May 2026
  • President Met with CEO of Palantir President of Ukraine 12 May 2026
  • Boris Pistorius visited a "small" air defense position to observe Ukraine's counter-Shahed system in action Ukraine MOD 12 May 2026 -- The German Defense Minister visited a "small" air defense position and an energy facility in Kyiv that had been targeted by Russian attacks.
  • AI and Ukraine's defence strategy: Mykhailo Fedorov and Palantir Technologies discuss cooperation to strengthen security Ukraine MOD 12 May 2026 -- Ukraine's Minister of Defence, Mykhailo Fedorov, held a meeting with Alex Karp, CEO of Palantir Technologies. The parties discussed opportunities for cooperation in the fields of artificial intelligence, data analysis and the scaling of technological solutions.

  • Soldiers fire up new skills on Balikatan Australia DOD 12 May 2026 -- In preparation for a live-fire serial during Exercise Balikatan, Australian gunners embedded with a United States high mobility artillery rocket system (HIMARS) battery exchanged lessons on tropical maintenance, launch preparation and turning sensor data into firing solutions.
  • Albanese Government delivering for Defence and our veterans Australia DOD 12 May 2026 -- The 2026-27 Budget delivers on the Albanese Labor Government's recently released 2026 National Defence Strategy and Integrated Investment Program outlining an additional $14 billion over the next four years and $53 billion over the decade that the government has invested in Defence in recognition of Australia's strategic environment.

  • Belarus conducts military command-staff exercise Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 12 May 2026 -- On 12-14 May, a command-staff exercise is being conducted in Belarus under the leadership of Deputy Defense Minister for Armament, Chief of Armament of the Armed Forces Andrei Fedin, the Belarusian Defense Ministry told BelTA.

  • IOS SAGAR DEPARTS CHATTOGRAM AFTER SUCCESSFUL PORT CALL IN BANGLADESH India PIB 12 May 2026 -- Indian Navy's INS Sunayna, deployed under the Indian Ocean Ship (IOS) SAGAR initiative, departed Chattogram, Bangladesh, on 10 May 2026 after a successful port call marked by professional engagements, maritime cooperation, and cultural exchanges with the Bangladesh Navy (BN). The ship is presently enroute to Colombo, Sri Lanka. The departure was marked by a ceremonial send-off by the Bangladesh Navy.

  • European Peace Facility: Council adopts the third bilateral assistance measure in support of the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina EC/CEU 12 May 2026 -- The Council today adopted an assistance measure worth €15 million under the European Peace Facility.

  • Foreign Affairs Council (Defence), 12 May 2026 EC/CEU 12 May 2026
  • Foreign Affairs Council (Defence): Press conference by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 May 2026
  • Foreign Affairs Council (Defence): Press remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas upon arrival European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 May 2026

  • Ministers of Defence pave the way for a stronger European Defence Agency European Defence Agency 12 May 2026 -- At the European Defence Agency's (EDA) Ministerial Steering Board meeting held today in Brussels, EU Ministers of Defence reviewed a single strategic agenda item: the strengthening of EDA.

  • United Kingdom A Cornerstone of OCCAR European Cooperation OCCAR 12 May 2026 -- The OCCAR (Organisation for Joint Armament Co-operation)-Executive Administration (EA) Senior Executive Team, Director Joachim Sucker and Head of Programmes Darren Ash, welcomed Lt Gen Anna-Lee Reilly CB FREng The National Armaments Director Group Director and Group Director General Material, to the OCCAR-EA Headquarters in Bonn.

  • The President: We offer to the defense industry a government that moves fast President of the Republic of Lithuania 12 May 2026 -- On Tuesday, President Gitanas Nausėda opened DAIMEX Baltic in Vilnius—the first international defense and security industry event of its kind in the Baltic region.
  • Address by Gitanas Nausėda, President of the Republic of Lithuania, at the DAIMEX Baltic 2026 Conference President of the Republic of Lithuania 12 May 2026 -- The President's Communication Group
  • Gitanas Nausėda discussed the U.S. role in European security and strengthening NATO air defense with Alexus G. Grynkewich President of the Republic of Lithuania 12 May 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda met with General Alexus G. Grynkewich, Supreme Allied Commander Europe and Commander of the United States European Command (EUCOM).

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 12 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Hamas warns of 'grave consequences' after Israeli law allows execution of Palestinian prisoners Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Hamas has warned of "grave consequences" following the passage of an Israeli law that establishes a special military court for the resistance group's members and grants it the authority to issue death sentences.
  • Israel passes law on death penalty for Palestinians over Operation Al-Aqsa Flood Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The Israeli Knesset has passed a new law imposing the death penalty and authorizing "show trials" for Palestinians accused of involvement in Operation Al-Aqsa Flood on October 7, 2023, based on evidence extracted under torture.

  • Hezbollah leader says resistance drones are tightening noose on Israel Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Hezbollah Secretary General Sheikh Naim Qassem has praised Lebanese resistance forces for their retaliatory drone operations against Israeli military positions, stressing that the unmanned aircraft are closely monitoring all enemy movements and are tightening the noose around Israeli occupation forces.
  • Overnight Israeli strike kills six civilians in southern Lebanon Press TV 12 May 2026 -- At least six people have been killed and seven others wounded after an overnight Israeli airstrike targeted a residential building in southern Lebanon, despite a ceasefire agreement being announced last month.
  • Israel Hit Over 1,100 Hezbollah Targets Since Ceasefire Began - IDF Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- Israel has struck over 1,100 targets belonging to Lebanese movement Hezbollah, killing more than 350 Hezbollah members since a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect on April 16, the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said on Tuesday.
  • Hezbollah Launches 20 Attacks on Israeli Forces Across Southern Lebanon Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- The Lebanese movement Hezbollah said on Tuesday that it had carried out 20 military operations against the Israeli army in southern Lebanon on Monday.
  • Israeli Air Force Attacks 38 Lebanese Settlements on Monday Sputnik 12 May 2026 -- Israeli warplanes attacked 38 settlements in southern and eastern Lebanon on Monday, a Lebanese military field source told Sputnik on Tuesday.

  • A demolition of homes, of schools, and of childhood UNICEF 12 May 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF spokesperson, James Elder - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva
  • Children shot, stabbed and pepper-sprayed in occupied West Bank UN News 12 May 2026 -- Israeli military operations and surging settler attacks in the occupied West Bank are killing and maiming a growing number of Palestinian children, while in Gaza tens of thousands with life-changing injuries lack access to treatment and rehabilitation, UN agencies warned on Tuesday.

  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Issues its 128th Report, Documenting 1,168 Crimes, Including the Demolition of 50 Commercial Structures and Land Grabbing Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 12 May 2026 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians recorded a higher level of Israeli crimes this week compared to previous weeks, totaling 1,168 crimes. Between May 5 and 11, 2026, Israeli occupation forces killed 19 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and injured 91 others.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine UNOCHA 12 May 2026 -- Medics killed, health centres hit despite ceasefire

  • 35 Chinese vessels swarm 4 key West PH Sea features Philippine News Agency 12 May 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Tuesday said around 35 Chinese naval and coast guard vessels were monitored in four key West Philippine Sea (WPS) features from May 4 to 11.

  • 2025 Syria Annual Protection Case Trends Update UNRWA 12 May 2026

  • In letter to UN, Yemen calls for end to blockade, sabotage by US, allies Press TV 12 May 2026 -- Yemen has written to the United Nations, calling for an end to over 10 years of blockade of the country and urging cessation of aggressive measures targeting the nation by the US and its allies.

  • Joint Declaration on Sudan - Adopted by the Joint Fact-Finding Mission of the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights on the Human Rights Situation in the Sudan and the United Nations Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan OHCHR 12 May 2026

  • Announcement of Targeted Visa Restrictions for Individuals Involved in Undermining Peace in South Sudan US Dept. of State 12 May 2026 -- The transitional government of South Sudan has, for years, impeded implementation of the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in the Republic of South Sudan (R-ARCSS), signed during President Trump's first term, leaving the country on the brink of a return to all-out war.

  • No ICC reentry plans yet, but Marcos open to future review Philippine News Agency 12 May 2026 -- Malacañang on Tuesday said President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. is not currently considering rejoining the International Criminal Court (ICC), but clarified that the administration is not permanently closing the door on future discussions regarding possible membership in the Rome Statute.

  • Bosnia and Herzegovina's future cannot be held hostage by divisive politics: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 12 May 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Bosnia & Herzegovina.

  • Chad's refugee crisis overwhelms maternity care in east, UN agency warns UN News 12 May 2026 -- Many women in eastern Chad are being forced to give birth in overcrowded clinics with limited medicine, minimal equipment and severe shortages of anesthesia, as a worsening humanitarian crisis overwhelms the country's fragile healthcare system, the UN reproductive health agency, UNFPA, warned on Tuesday.

  • Bosnia and Herzegovina Must Choose between Strong Institutions, Stagnation, High Representative Says as Security Council Delegates Urge Respect for Dayton Accord UN Security Council 12 May 2026 -- Bosnia and Herzegovina is moving along "a narrowing path" that could lead either towards institutional sustainability and economic revitalization, or stagnation and deconstruction of State institutions, the Security Council heard today from the High Representative of the international community in that country.

News Reports

  • US releasing 53mn barrels from strategic oil reserve to mitigate fallout from war on Iran Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The United States is releasing 53.3 million barrels of crude oil from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve to energy companies in an attempt to calm global markets shaken by the war against Iran and the disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Another Member of Notorious Philadelphia '10th and O Crew' Sentenced to 60 Months for Opioid Drug Conspiracy US Dept. of Justice 12 May 2026 -- A Pennsylvania man was sentenced today in the District of New Jersey to 60 months in prison for conspiracy to distribute oxycodone, a highly addictive controlled substance.

  • Announcing Steps to Impose Visa Restrictions on Associates of Illicit Fentanyl Traffickers US Dept. of State 12 May 2026 -- Illicit fentanyl is killing too many Americans. Sanctioned online pharmacies like KS International Traders, based in India, have sold hundreds of thousands of counterfeit prescription pills laced with illicit fentanyl to unsuspecting victims across the United States, devastating families and communities nationwide.

  • Meeting with Human Rights Commissioner Tatyana Moskalkova President of Russia 12 May 2026 -- Tatyana Moskalkova presented to the President her annual report as Human Rights Commissioner of the Russian Federation and summed up the results of her ten years in office. The discussion focused on prisoner exchanges involving Russian soldiers, humanitarian assistance for prisoners of war, and the return of displaced civilians, including children.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 12, 2026 PRC MofA 12 May 2026
  • Iran, Taiwan, And Trade Tensions: What To Expect At The Trump-Xi Summit RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping are set to open a closely watched summit in Beijing as Washington and Beijing seek a fragile economic truce while maneuvering over Iran, Taiwan, and control of critical supply chains.
  • Perspective: Be wary of China twisting Trump's words during summit with Xi Jinping Radio Free Asia 12 May 2026 -- During U.S. President Donald Trump's visit to China, pay special attention to China's track record of manipulating information. The Chinese government has distorted the statements of foreign visitors and used them to serve its own rule over the people under its control.
  • Chinese FM addresses question about US President's remarks on discussing US arms sales to Taiwan, Jimmy Lai case with Chinese side Global Times 12 May 2026 -- During US President Donald Trump's visit to China, the two heads of state will have in-depth exchanges of views on major issues concerning China-US relations as well as world peace and development, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday in response to a question about US President Trump said on Monday that he would discuss US arms sales to Taiwan and the case of Jimmy Lai with Chinese leader and question about whether China is prepared to engage in dialogue on these major issues.
  • Xi holds talks with Tajik president PRC MND 12 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping held talks with Tajikistan's President Emomali Rahmon in Beijing on Tuesday.
  • President Xi Jinping Holds Talks with Tajik President Emomali Rahmon PRC MofA 12 May 2026 -- On the afternoon of May 12, President Xi Jinping held talks with Tajik President Emomali Rahmon, who is on a state visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Xi meets Brunei's crown prince, calling for deepening practical cooperation PRC MND 12 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Haji Al-Muhtadee Billah, the Crown Prince and Senior Minister at the Prime Minister's Office of Brunei Darussalam, in Beijing on Tuesday, calling for strengthening strategic communication and deepening practical cooperation.
  • President Xi Jinping Meets with Crown Prince of Brunei Haji Al-Muhtadee Billah PRC MofA 12 May 2026 -- On the afternoon of May 12, 2026, President Xi Jinping met with Crown Prince of Brunei Haji Al-Muhtadee Billah, who is on an official visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.
  • Central govt firmly supports HK's judicial authorities in performing their duties in line with the law: FM responds to question about whether China considers releasing Jimmy Lai Global Times 12 May 2026 -- Jimmy Lai was a major organizer and participant in anti-China and destabilizing activities in Hong Kong, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday in response to a question about whether China is considering releasing Jimmy Lai.

  • Obstruction of Eswatini visit proof of consolidating authoritarianism: Lai CNA 12 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德), speaking at the Copenhagen Democracy Summit Tuesday, cited China's alleged obstruction of his recent state visit to Eswatini, Taiwan's only African ally, as evidence of "consolidating" authoritarian regimes worldwide.
  • Taiwan condemns China over WHA participation 'non-approval' comments CNA 12 May 2026 -- Taiwan has condemned China for saying that it had decided not to approve Taipei's participation in the upcoming World Health Assembly (WHA) because Beijing sees Taipei as part of its territory.
  • China firmly opposes and condemns Paraguayan relevant moves concerning Taiwan region, urges Paraguayan government to recognize one-China principle: FM Global Times 12 May 2026 -- When asked to comment on President of Paraguay Santiago Pena's visit to Taiwan region from May 7 to 10, during which he met with Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te and signed several so-called "cooperation documents," and made claims including that Paraguay supported Taiwan's "participation in international multilateral systems," "the international community should recognize Taiwan," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday that China firmly opposes and condemns such moves made by the Paraguayan side.
  • ICYMI: Ranking Member Shaheen Leads Congressional Push for Taiwan Support Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 12 May 2026 -- Yesterday, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, led the charge from Congress to push President Trump to not sell out American national security during his summit with Chinese President Xi in Beijing later this week.
  • MOFA mum on whether Trump arms sales remarks break Six Assurances CNA 12 May 2026 -- Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) on Tuesday declined to directly answer questions about whether U.S. President Donald Trump's remarks about discussing arms sales to Taiwan with Chinese leader Xi Jinping (習近平) would violate a longstanding U.S. policy toward Taiwan.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 12 May 2026

  • Kuwait Announces Arrest Of 4 Individuals 'Affiliated With IRGC' RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- Kuwait's Interior Ministry announced that it had arrested "four infiltrators" affiliated with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) after they attempted to enter the Persian Gulf country by sea.
  • IRGC dismantles weapons smuggling networks affiliated with Israeli regime Press TV 12 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) intelligence unit has dismantled five arms and ammunition smuggling networks affiliated with the Israeli regime.
  • Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi In Critical Condition After Transfer To Tehran Hospital RFE/RL 12 May 2026 -- The brother of imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi says doctors cannot yet offer a definitive prognosis on her condition, days after Iranian authorities released her on bail amid a health crisis that her family says nearly killed her.

  • 'Gray rhino' of Japan's remilitarization is running toward us, Chinese FM warns amid Tokyo's security policy shift Global Times 12 May 2026 -- The "gray rhino" of Japan's remilitarization is running toward us. Many international experts, scholars and media have noted that Japan has abandoned pacifism, and is undergoing its most dangerous shift in military and security policy since WWII, posing great threats to peace and stability in the region and beyond, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Tuesday.

  • During a Lecture to the UAE National Defense College Members, HE GCCSG Reaffirms the GCC States as Firm Strategic Pillar Amid Rapid Geopolitical Shifts; They Exert Significant Efforts to Anticipate Opportunities and Confront Challenges Gulf Cooperation Council 12 May 2026

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Can Ukraine And Moldova Finally Start Accession Talks? RFE/RL 12 May 2026

  • World News in Brief: Conflict drives hunger in DR Congo, mass corporal punishment in Afghanistan, Earth's sand is running out UN News 12 May 2026 -- More than 26.5 million people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo are facing acute hunger, according to a new analysis from UN-backed food security experts published Tuesday.
